checks.a_analysis: The output text has been thoroughly checked against the source document. All headings, paragraph text, table contents, and lists correspond to information present in the source PDF. No hallucinated content or fabricated sections were identified. The metadata title and image alt text are acceptable pipeline-generated additions.

I verified all names and labels in the document. The only URL in the source (https://it.nc.gov/gicc) is missing from the output, rather than being incorrectly represented or fabricated. No new phone numbers, emails, or URLs were added.

All main components of the one-page source document (Header, Agenda table, Committee details, Meeting dates, and Footer label) are present in the output. While a specific piece of text (the URL) is missing, it does not constitute an entire section or page.

The source table contains a header row and 6 numbered agenda item rows (Total 7 rows). The JSON output table (reading_order 7) correctly identifies a header row and 6 data rows, maintaining a 1:1 row count for the primary agenda items.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The URL address 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' following the label 'GICC Website:' is completely missing from the output. In the JSON, the paragraph at reading_order 12 only contains the prefix text 'GICC Website: ', and there is no subsequent text element for the actual URL (there is a gap in the reading_order between 12 and 14 where the URL should be). As this is the primary contact/resource URL for the council, its omission is a significant loss of information.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The existing text content accurately reflects the source wording. Structural changes, such as moving the sub-list of committees and names out of the table into separate list and paragraph elements (reading_order 8 and 9), are acceptable reformatting and preserve the original information.

checks.a_analysis: The content from the source PDF appears only once in the JSON output. There are no duplicated paragraphs, lists, or table rows.
The heading hierarchy follows a logical structure. H1 is used for the organization name, H2 for the document type (AGENDA), and H3 for the meeting dates section. No levels are skipped.

The table structure is significantly corrupted. In the source, Item 5 ('Committee Reports') includes a bulleted list in the 'AGENDA ITEM' column and a list of names in the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column. In the JSON (element 7), Row 5 (Item 5) is missing its sub-list content and the third column is an empty string. Furthermore, Row 6 (Item 6) is included within the table structure in element 7, while the details for Item 5 are extracted as separate elements (8 and 9) following the table.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5 in table: 'Committee Reports' followed by a bulleted list of committees and a corresponding list of member names in the third column.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element index 7, Row index 5: 'content': [{"text": "5. Committee Reports"}], 'cells'[2]: {"cell_type": "data", "content": [{"text": ""}]}

checks.a_analysis: The reading order is logically broken due to the structural failure in Check S3. The table (element 7) includes Item 6 ('GICC Member Announcements'). The details for Item 5 ('Committee Reports' list and members) are placed in elements 8 and 9. Consequently, a reader navigating top-to-bottom will encounter the summary of Item 6 before the specific details of Item 5.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5 details (Committee list and members) are logically followed by Item 6 (Member Announcements).
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 7 (Reading Order 7) contains Item 6. Element 8 and 9 (Reading Order 8 and 9) contain Item 5 details.
checks.b_evidence_location: Main agenda table and subsequent list/paragraph, elements 7, 8, and 9

checks.a_analysis: The bulleted list of committee reports in the source is correctly identified as an unordered list in element 8. While its position is incorrect (outside the table), the list itself is well-formed.
The document contains a URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' in the footer. In the output (element 12), the text 'GICC Website: ' is present, but the actual URL is missing entirely from the JSON data.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of information in the HTML output differs significantly from the source PDF regarding item 5 of the agenda. In the source PDF, the committee reports and their respective presenters are organized within the table, maintaining a clear visual association. In the HTML output, the content for item 5 has been extracted and reorganized into a bulleted list and a string of names positioned below the table. This breaks the visual flow and organization of the agenda information.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF shows '5. Committee Reports' within the table, with sub-bullets for committees in the 'AGENDA ITEM' column and names in the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, the table row for item 5 is mostly empty, and the sub-bullets and names are rendered as separate text blocks below the table frame.
checks.b_evidence_location: Item 5 of the Agenda Table, Page 1

checks.a_analysis: The agenda table is visually broken because the content of row 5 has been improperly handled. The source document lists presenters in the right-hand column corresponding to specific committees in the middle column. The HTML output renders the row with an empty right column and places the presenters' names in a single, unformatted line of text below the table. This makes the table data for this section unreadable as a structured set of information, as the association between the committee and the member is lost.
checks.b_evidence_source: A table with three columns ('TIME', 'AGENDA ITEM', 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST'). Row 5 contains a list of 6 committees and 6 corresponding names in the right column.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table has row 5 as '15 min. | 5. Committee Reports | [empty]'. The associated names 'Paul Badr Debbie Brannan Scott Lokken Dean Grantham John Farley Alex Rankin' appear as a flat string of text below the table.

checks.a_analysis: The meaningful NCGICC logo is present in both the source PDF and the HTML output. Although its position has moved from the top header to the bottom of the content area in the HTML rendering, it is correctly displayed.

The content appears only once in the body of the HTML output. While section headings appear in the 'Contents' navigation sidebar and the main body, this is an intentional accessibility feature and not a defect.
